para_trans: Those working in the category of deaf and mute
Subsidized Grants to Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs)
regarding the distribution of funds.
Kishore Appa Multipurpose Institute, Pachora
Residential Deaf School Pachora, Dist. Jalgaon
Government of Maharashtra
Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
Government Memorandum, No: - Divyang - 2026 / Q. No.50 / E-1600775 / Work-9
Date: 28th March, 2026
Reference: - 1. Government Circular No. Divyang - 2022 / P.S. No.10 / D. C. 2, D. 13th September, 2022.
2. Department No. Disability - 2024 / P.No.135 / DC-2, dated 27th September, 2023.
3. Commissioner, Divyang Welfare, Pune. Letter from 26.02.2026 (1744418).
The memo: -

Reference No. 1 to the prescribed procedure to be followed before payment of salaries and non-salaried dues of teachers / non-teaching staff in Special Non-Government Schools / Workshops for Persons with Disabilities. The directions have been issued vide Government Circular No.1 dated 13th September, 2022 in Reference No. Instructions have been issued for scrutiny and submission of proposals as per the Scrutiny List prescribed vide letter dated 27.09.2023.

Accordingly, as per the non-payment proposal of Kishore Appa Multipurpose Institute Pachora-run Residential Deaf School Pachora, Dist. Jalgaon received by the Department from the Commissioner, Divyang Kalyan, Pune, funds are being disbursed for grant-in-aid (Programme), 31-Auxiliary Grants (Non-Payment) to Non-Governmental Organizations working for the Persons with Disabilities category as follows.
(Amount in thousands)
table_trans: Barrister Rajni Patel Marg, Nariman Point,
Name and address of non-government aided special schools / workshops etc.
1. Kishore Appa Multipurpose Institute Pachora Operated Residential Deaf School Pachora, Dist. Jalgaon

para_trans: The Commissioner, Commissionerate of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Maharashtra State, Pune will be the controlling officer to control the expenditure of the said fund. While disbursing the said funds, ensure that the proposal for non-salaried grants is sound.
Only then should the process of disbursing funds to the respective activities be carried out.

4. Proceedings for disbursement of funds by verifying that the penalty is applicable to the activity concerned or how and if applicable, the penalty has been imposed as per Section No. 5, Auxiliary Grants, Part-I, Roll No. 77 (nine) of the School Code.
should be done.

The Controlling Officer shall control the expenditure of the funds in section 4 of the aforesaid statement and disburse the funds subject to furnishing of utilization certificates, statement of expenditure and statement of expenditure.
The utility certificate should be submitted to the government without fail within 1 month.
6. Maharashtra Budget Manual, School Code and Fiscal while the Controller General of Expenditure.

The guidelines and norms in force and issued by the Planning Department, Finance Department as well as this Department from time to time, the delegations of financial powers as well as the instructions given from time to time are mentioned in the Rights Manual.
Follow strictly.
7. Funds / grants may not be spent for any purpose other than the purpose for which they are sanctioned. Funds cannot be diverted to other headings.
The memorandum was made available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra, www.maharashtra.gov.in.
